content.json:
# TRAP ↔ MDO Parallel Run

The Phase 2 decision workbook. Run TRAP and the MDO OOTB deployment side-by-side over a representative incident window, then use the numbers below to decide which Phase 3 enhancements you actually need.

The MDO panels query Sentinel directly. The **TRAP-side numbers are not in Sentinel** unless you import them via a custom log. Until you do, fill the TRAP column on a daily standup spreadsheet and compare visually — most teams that ran this exercise found the spreadsheet sufficient and never imported TRAP data into Sentinel.

Decision criteria are in [`11-implementation-roadmap.md`](../../11-implementation-roadmap.md) Phase 2.

## 1. User-reported phish volume

Reporters using the legacy PhishAlarm button still flow into TRAP. Reporters using the built-in Outlook Report button flow into MDO/Sentinel.

The MDO side is below. Track the PhishAlarm/TRAP side daily and compare — you're looking for the MDO side to ramp as users find the new button, and TRAP volume to taper. By the end of Phase 2 the MDO side should dominate.

content.query:
AlertInfo
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where ServiceSource == 'Microsoft Defender for Office 365'
| where Title has 'Email reported by user'
| summarize ReportsViaMdo = count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 1d)
| render timechart with (title='MDO side — user reports per day')
content.title: MDO user-reports per day

content.json:
## 2. MTTR — mean time to remediate

For MDO this is the gap from incident creation to first remediation action (`OfficeActivity` Soft/HardDelete or Set-MailItem). For TRAP this is the equivalent gap in its own UI — you log this manually during the parallel-run period.

The MDO side here is dominated by AIR investigation latency plus Take Action fan-out, both of which are Microsoft-managed and bounded.

content.query:
let incidents = SecurityIncident
    | where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
    | where Title has_any ('Email reported','Phish delivered','suspicious email','Email messages')
    | summarize CreatedAt = min(TimeGenerated) by IncidentNumber;
let remediations = OfficeActivity
    | where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
    | where Operation in ('HardDelete','SoftDelete','MoveToDeletedItems','Set-MailItem')
    | summarize FirstActionAt = min(TimeGenerated) by tostring(ClientIP)
    | project FirstActionAt;
incidents
| extend FirstActionAt = toscalar(remediations | summarize max(FirstActionAt))
| extend MinutesToRemediate = datetime_diff('minute', FirstActionAt, CreatedAt)
| summarize MttrMinutes = percentile(MinutesToRemediate, 50), p90 = percentile(MinutesToRemediate, 90), Total = count()
| project MttrMinutes_p50 = MttrMinutes, MttrMinutes_p90 = p90, IncidentsObserved = Total
content.title: MDO MTTR (minutes)

content.json:
## 3. False positive rate

Quarantine releases (admin OR self-service) are the canonical FP signal. A high release rate means the policies are too aggressive; a near-zero release rate means policies are well-tuned (or users are not finding the quarantine — verify by sampling).

content.query:
OfficeActivity
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where Operation in ('ReleaseQuarantinedItem','SubmitToMicrosoft','Set-QuarantinedMessage')
| summarize Releases = count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 1d)
| render columnchart with (title='Quarantine releases per day')
content.title: Quarantine releases per day

content.json:
## 4. AIR investigation outcomes

For every user-reported phish, the verdict the AIR engine produced. A healthy distribution looks like ~40% TruePositive, ~30% FalsePositive, ~30% Inconclusive. Heavy Inconclusive suggests the AIR engine isn't getting enough signal — tune anti-phish and look at why.

This panel only populates when the M365 Defender Investigations table is enabled on the connector (see Phase 1 task #7).

content.query:
AlertInfo
| where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
| where ServiceSource == 'Microsoft Defender for Office 365'
| where Title has 'Email reported by user'
| summarize ReportsTotal = count() by AlertId
| join kind=leftouter (
    AlertEvidence
    | where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
    | where EntityType == 'CloudLogonSession' or EntityType == 'Process'
    | project AlertId, Verdict = RemediationStatus
) on AlertId
| extend Verdict = coalesce(Verdict, 'Unknown')
| summarize Count = sum(ReportsTotal) by Verdict
| order by Count desc
content.title: AIR verdict mix

content.json:
## 5. Phase-2 gap candidates

The incidents below are the ones where the OOTB MDO deployment has the most chance of underperforming TRAP. If any of these rows show a non-trivial count, it's a candidate for a Phase 3 playbook:

* Older-than-48h delivered phish with no remediation → **P2 (TI Sweep)**.
* Phish delivered to a DL with multiple downstream recipients → **P5 (DL Expand)**.
* Phish that produced an internal forward (Intraorg subsequent message) → **P4 (Forward Trace)**.

content.query:
let candidates = EmailEvents
    | where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
    | where EmailDirection == 'Inbound'
    | where ThreatTypes has_any ('Phish','Malware')
    | where DeliveryAction == 'Delivered'
    | project Timestamp, NetworkMessageId, RecipientEmailAddress, SenderFromAddress, Subject, ThreatTypes;
let remediated = OfficeActivity
    | where TimeGenerated {TimeRange}
    | where Operation in ('HardDelete','SoftDelete')
    | project ActionTime = TimeGenerated, RecipientEmailAddress = tostring(parse_json(MessageId).RecipientEmail);
candidates
| join kind=leftouter remediated on RecipientEmailAddress
| extend Status = case(isnotempty(ActionTime), 'Remediated', 'StillDelivered')
| summarize Threats = count() by Status, Outcome = case(
    datetime_diff('hour', now(), Timestamp) > 48 and Status == 'StillDelivered', 'A. >48h, no remediation (P2 candidate)',
    Status == 'StillDelivered', 'B. ≤48h, still delivered',
    Status == 'Remediated', 'C. Remediated',
    'D. Other')
| order by Outcome asc
content.title: Gap-candidate breakdown

content.json:
## 6. Decision template

Fill these rows daily during the parallel-run period. After 2–4 weeks of data, the bottom row tells you whether Phase 3 enhancements are needed.

| Metric | TRAP | MDO | Delta (MDO - TRAP) | Acceptable? |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Daily reported phish | _from PhishAlarm UI_ | from panel 1 above | | |
| MTTR median (min) | _from TRAP UI_ | from panel 2 above | | |
| FP rate (releases/total) | _from TRAP UI_ | from panel 3 above | | |
| Coverage gaps (>48h) | _N/A_ | from panel 5 above | | |

Exit criteria: MDO matches-or-beats TRAP on the first three rows AND the count of P2/P4/P5 candidates in panel 5 is small enough that the SOC accepts losing them OR you commit to building the corresponding Phase 3 playbook before Phase 4 cutover.
